stages.name: Beginning
stages.summary: Security is reactive. Controls exist where required, not where risk lives.
challenges: No formal security ownership
Ad-hoc tooling with no unified visibility
Manual processes for most security tasks
Reactive response to incidents, not prevention

stages.name: Growing
stages.summary: Security investment has started, but coverage is inconsistent across the environment.
challenges: More cloud, more users, more applications
Limited visibility across expanding environment
Manual processes that don't scale
Point solutions without an underlying program

stages.name: Scaling
stages.summary: Core controls are in place. The focus shifts to consistency, automation, and coverage gaps.
challenges: Inconsistent enforcement across business units
Growing complexity outpacing the security team
Need for automation to keep pace with scale
Emerging compliance and audit pressure

stages.name: Optimizing
stages.summary: Mature controls exist. The focus is tuning, reducing noise, and proving effectiveness.
challenges: Alert fatigue and detection tuning
Proving control effectiveness to leadership
Reducing operational overhead
Closing residual gaps from rapid early growth

stages.name: Continuous Improvement
stages.summary: Security is a continuous discipline, not a project. The program adapts as the business changes.
challenges: Keeping pace with a changing threat landscape
Sustaining maturity through organizational change
Quarterly validation and strategic planning
Demonstrating ongoing ROI on the security program
